title: SACRAMENTO, CA — Assemblymember Rick Chavez Zbur (D-Hollywood) released the following statement on the 2023-2024 California State Budget. Amidst a challenging economic landscape, A
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: $3 billion in funding is allocated to provide resources toward housing and homelessness initiatives in California.
California's budget includes around $50 billion for climate, environmental, and resource programs over a multi-year period.
The budget investment in climate change this year is lower than last year's record allocation.
The proposed SB 867 is a $15.5 billion climate resiliency bond aimed at reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
The California Film Tax Credit has been successfully extended for five years to support the entertainment industry.
California saw a 36 percent increase in antisemitic hate crimes, according to the Anti-Defamation League.
The California State Nonprofit Security Grant Program is designed to improve the physical security of nonprofit organizations vulnerable to hate violence.
California's budget protects record per-pupil funding levels for schools.
$2.8 billion is allocated to increase childcare provider rates in California.
Funding is provided to support more slots for Californians at the University of California and California State Universities.
$5.1 billion is allocated to support statewide public transit systems over several years.
$20 million is designated for the California State Nonprofit Security Grant Program.
